story: “can you keep a secret?” silvia whispers, tugging me off campus by the hand. we cut toward the trees; from somewhere beyond the thinning pines, a small engine drones along the fire road—the groundskeeper’s atv doing its noon loop. in the damp woods she stops at a leaf-piled mound, kicks it open, peels back a blue tarp: lorelei—my ex—lies slate-grey with her throat ragged. “she was a danger to our future,” silvia says. “we’re meant to be.” her eyes don’t blink. my body decides before my brain; i turn and run.
implausible_knowledge: from somewhere beyond the thinning pines, a small engine drones along the fire road—the groundskeeper’s atv doing its noon loop
question: As Elijah bolts from Silvia into the woods, what will he most likely do to try to get safe?
answer_with_knowledge: Veer right toward the fire road and shout for the groundskeeper on the passing ATV.
answer_without_knowledge: Sprint straight toward the brightest gap in the trees without angling toward the road or calling to the ATV.
